exercises.title: Recovery Table Builder
exercises.description: Build a complete recovery table for stabilizer codes.
tasks: Enumerate all single-qubit errors (X, Y, Z on each qubit)
Compute syndrome for each error
Build syndrome → correction mapping
Handle collisions (same syndrome for different errors)
Test on 5-qubit and Steane codes
exercises.expectedOutput: Complete recovery table with collision report
hints: Perfect code: no collisions for correctable errors

exercises.title: QEC Cycle Simulator
exercises.description: Simulate the complete quantum error correction cycle.
tasks: Implement encoding of logical states
Apply random errors with given probability
Extract syndrome via stabilizer measurements
Apply recovery based on syndrome
Verify final state matches initial encoded state
hints: Use fidelity to check recovery success
Random error: choose qubit and Pauli type
Track logical error rate over many trials

exercises.title: Degeneracy Analyzer
exercises.description: Analyze degeneracy in quantum error-correcting codes.
tasks: Find all error pairs with same syndrome
Check if their product is a stabilizer
Classify as degenerate or distinct errors
Compare degeneracy in Shor, Steane, and 5-qubit codes
Explain impact on effective distance
hints: E₁†E₂ = E₁E₂ for Paulis (since E† = E)

exercises.title: Logical Error Rate Estimator
exercises.description: Estimate logical error rate under depolarizing noise.
tasks: Simulate many QEC cycles with random errors
Count logical errors (failure to recover)
Plot logical vs physical error rate
Find pseudo-threshold for the code
Compare different codes at same physical rate
exercises.expectedOutput: Logical error rate analysis with plots
hints: Depolarizing: X, Y, Z each with probability p/3
Logical error when R×E is logical operator
For d=3: p_L ≈ C×p² for small p
